author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
Another Interpretation of the Apostolic Passage, Who Will Have All Men to Be Saved.
title: Another Interpretation of the Apostolic Passage, Who Will Have All Men to Be Saved.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: That, therefore, in our ignorance of who shall be saved, God commands us to will that all to whom we preach this peace may be saved, and Himself works this in us by diffusing that love in our hearts by the Holy Spirit who is given to us,—may also thus be understood, that God wills all men to be saved, because He makes us to will this; just as “He sent the Spirit of His Son, crying, Abba, Father;” that is, making us to cry, Abba, Father.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: Because, concerning that same Spirit, He says in another place, “We have received the Spirit of adoption, in whom we cry, Abba, Father!”

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: We therefore cry, but He is said to cry who makes us to cry.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: If, then, Scripture rightly said that the Spirit was crying by whom we are made to cry, it rightly also says that God wills, when by Him we are made to will.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: And thus, because by rebuke we ought to do nothing save to avoid departure from that peace which is towards God, or to induce return to it of him who had departed, let us do in hope what we do.

verses.book: A Treatise on Rebuke and Grace.
verses.text: If he whom we rebuke is a son of peace, our peace shall rest upon him; but if not, it shall return to us again.
